title: quercus insignis subsp. oocarpa
intro: Quercus insignis subsp. oocarpa, also known as the Oocarpa Oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree in the Quercus genus, native to Central America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The Oocarpa Oak is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of Quercus insignis subsp. oocarpa typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures made from the leaves or bark, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
